title: Splashing Into National Dolphin Day
story: Every April 14, people around the world celebrate National Dolphin Day to honor these amazing ocean athletes! Aquariums host special dolphin shows where visitors can watch these smart mammals leap, spin, and play games with their trainers. Marine biologists share cool facts about how dolphins talk to each other using clicks and whistles, almost like they have their own secret language. Many families visit beaches hoping to spot wild dolphins jumping in the waves. Schools often show dolphin documentaries, and kids love learning that dolphins recognize themselves in mirrors, just like humans do. Social media fills with dolphin videos and photos as people share their favorite flippered friends to spread awareness about protecting ocean habitats.
activity_question: What do dolphins use to communicate with each other?
bonus_fact: Dolphins have names for each other - unique whistle signatures!

title: Going Nuts for National Pecan Day!
story: Every April 14, pecan lovers across America celebrate National Pecan Day by cracking open these buttery nuts! People bake pecan pies, make pralines, and snack on roasted pecans. Many families share their favorite pecan recipes on social media, while others visit pecan orchards for tours and tastings. Some folks even have pecan-cracking contests to see who can get the biggest whole piece out of the shell!
activity_question: What do people often bake to celebrate National Pecan Day?
bonus_fact: It takes about 7 years for a pecan tree to produce its first nuts!

title: Stretch Your Dreams Sky High!
story: People everywhere stretch their arms up as high as possible on April 14 for National Reach as High as You Can Day! This fun holiday encourages everyone to literally reach toward the sky while also thinking about their biggest dreams and goals. Kids and adults take photos of themselves stretching upward in parks, playgrounds, and even their own backyards. Some people climb trees, jump with their arms up, or stand on tippy-toes to get extra height. It's a day to remember that reaching high physically can inspire you to reach high in life too!
activity_question: What do people do with their arms on National Reach as High as You Can Day?
activity_choices: Stretch them up as high as possible
activity_answer: Stretch them up as high as possible
follow_up_question: What's the highest goal or dream you'd like to reach for in your life?

title: Sky's the Limit on Look Up Day!
story: Every April 14, people celebrate National Look Up at the Sky Day by doing exactly what it sounds like - looking up! Families step outside and gaze at clouds, search for birds, or spot airplanes flying overhead. Some people bring blankets to lie on the grass and watch cloud shapes change. Others use telescopes or binoculars to get a closer look at interesting things above them. It's a perfect excuse to put down phones and tablets and enjoy nature's free entertainment right above our heads!
activity_question: What do some people bring outside to help them look at the sky more comfortably?
follow_up_question: What's the most interesting thing you've ever spotted in the sky?

title: Giggle Your Way Through Laughter Day
story: Every April 14, people across the country celebrate National Moment of Laughter Day by doing exactly what it sounds like - laughing! This special day encourages everyone to find joy and share giggles with friends, family, and even strangers. People tell their favorite jokes, watch funny movies, share silly memes online, and remember embarrassing moments that now seem hilarious. Schools might have joke-telling contests, offices share funny stories during lunch breaks, and families gather to laugh together. The best part? Laughter is contagious, so one person's chuckle can spread smiles everywhere they go!
activity_question: What do people share online during National Moment of Laughter Day?
bonus_fact: Scientists say laughing for just 15 minutes can burn as many calories as walking!
